perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its officers, employees (together, ‘Authorised Persons’) who : reasonably need to receive the Confidential Information to enable the Receiving Party to achieve the Permitted Purpose; have been informed by the Receiving Party (a) of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and (b) that the Disclosing Party provided the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party subject to the provisions of a written confidentiality agreement; in the case of the Receiving Party’s officers and employees, have written confidentiality obligations to the Receiving P arty that (a) are no less onerous than the provisions of this Agreement and (b) apply to the Confidential Information, and who have been instructed to treat the Confidential Information as confidential; have been provided with a copy of this Agreement and have agreed with the Receiving Party in writing to comply with the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, and that agreement provides that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to enforce the agreement as a third -party beneficiary; a nd in the case of the Receiving Party’s solicitors, have confirmed that they will treat the Confidential Information as if it were the Receiving Party’s confidential information and therefore subject to the rules of the Law Society concerning client infor mation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its officers, employees (together, ‘Authorised Persons’) who : reasonably need to receive the Confidential Information to enable the Receiving Party to achieve the Permitted Purpose; have been informed by the Receiving Party (a) of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and (b) that the Disclosing Party provided the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party subject to the provisions of a written confidentiality agreement; in the case of the Receiving Party’s officers and employees, **do not** have written confidentiality obligations to the Receiving P arty that (a) are no less onerous than the provisions of this Agreement and (b) apply to the Confidential Information, and who have been instructed to treat the Confidential Information as confidential; have been provided with a copy of this Agreement and have agreed with the Receiving Party in writing to comply with the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, and that agreement provides that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to enforce the agreement as a third -party beneficiary; a nd in the case of the Receiving Party’s solicitors, have confirmed that they will treat the Confidential Information as if it were the Receiving Party’s confidential information and therefore subject to the rules of the Law Society concerning client infor mation.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of 'do not' creates a direct contradiction. Originally, authorised persons 'have written confidentiality obligations', now it's stated they 'do not'. This creates ambiguity as to whether employees must adhere to a written confidentiality agreement, especially considering that the agreement provides that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to enforce the agreement as a third -party beneficiary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its officers, employees (together, ‘Authorised Persons’) who : reasonably need to receive the Confidential Information to enable the Receiving Party to achieve the Permitted Purpose; have been informed by the Receiving Party (a) of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and (b) that the Disclosing Party provided the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party subject to the provisions of a written confidentiality agreement; in the case of the Receiving Party’s officers and employees, **do not** have written confidentiality obligations to the Receiving P arty that (a) are no less onerous than the provisions of this Agreement and (b) apply to the Confidential Information, and who have been instructed to treat the Confidential Information as confidential; have been provided with a copy of this Agreement and have agreed with the Receiving Party in writing to comply with the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, and that agreement provides that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to enforce the agreement as a third -party beneficiary; a nd in the case of the Receiving Party’s solicitors, have confirmed that they will treat the Confidential Information as if it were the Receiving Party’s confidential information and therefore subject to the rules of the Law Society concerning client infor mation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that authorised persons 'have written confidentiality obligations,' while the changed text states that they 'do not have written confidentiality obligations'. This directly conflicts with other parts of the document (the 'Contradicted Text', which is located at the same place as the changed text) and creates a strong ambiguity as to whether employees must adhere to a written confidentiality agreement. This makes the contract inconsistent and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to clause 6.2, the Receiving Party shall (a) at the Disclosing Party’s request, and al so (b) upon any termination of this Agreement: Return and provide to the Disclosing Party all documents and other materials that contain any of the Confidential Information, including all copies made by the Receiving Party representatives; Permanently del ete all electronic copies of Confidential Information from the Receiving Party’s computer systems; and Provide to the Disclosing Party a certificate, signed by an officer of the Receiving Party, confirming that the obligations referred to in clause 6.1 h as been met.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to clause 6.2, the Receiving Party shall (a) at the Disclosing Party’s request, and al so (b) upon any termination of this Agreement: **Maintain** and provide to the Disclosing Party all documents and other materials that contain any of the Confidential Information, including all copies made by the Receiving Party representatives; Permanently del ete all electronic copies of Confidential Information from the Receiving Party’s computer systems; and Provide to the Disclosing Party a certificate, signed by an officer of the Receiving Party, confirming that the obligations referred to in clause 6.1 h as been met.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Return' to 'Maintain' introduces a direct contradiction. The clause now instructs the Receiving Party to both maintain AND return all confidential information. This conflict creates uncertainty about the Receiving Party's actual obligation upon termination, which conflicts the statement: Provide to the Disclosing Party a certificate, signed by an officer of the Receiving Party, confirming that the obligations referred to in clause 6.1 h as been met.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to clause 6.2, the Receiving Party shall (a) at the Disclosing Party’s request, and al so (b) upon any termination of this Agreement: **Maintain** and provide to the Disclosing Party all documents and other materials that contain any of the Confidential Information, including all copies made by the Receiving Party representatives; Permanently del ete all electronic copies of Confidential Information from the Receiving Party’s computer systems; and Provide to the Disclosing Party a certificate, signed by an officer of the Receiving Party, confirming that the obligations referred to in clause 6.1 h as been met.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Return' to 'Maintain' introduces a direct contradiction. The clause now instructs the Receiving Party to both maintain AND return all confidential information, creating uncertainty about the Receiving Party's actual obligation upon termination. This contradiction is further highlighted by the subsequent requirement to provide a certificate confirming obligations under clause 6.1 have been met, which is now unclear due to the conflicting instructions. The changed text directly conflicts with the contradicted text, satisfying the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: ‘Information’ shall include information wheth er of a technical, commercial or any other nature whatsoever provided directly or indirectly by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party in oral or documentary form or by way of models, biological or chemical materials or other tangible form or by dem onstrations and whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: ‘Information’ shall include information wheth er of a technical, commercial or any other nature whatsoever provided directly or indirectly by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party in oral or documentary form or by way of models, biological or chemical materials or other tangible form or by dem onstrations and whether **only** before, on or after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'only' restricts the timeframe for what qualifies as 'Information' under the agreement, from the original broad definition, to one that is only considered under that definition before, on, or after the agreement. This contrasts with clause 2 where it states: In consideration of the Disclosing Party providing Confidential Information, at its discretion , to the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party shall: Keep the Confidential Information secret and confidential
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Disclosing Party providing Confidential Information, at its discretion , to the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party shall: Keep the Confidential Information secret and confidential; Neither disclose nor permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any person, except for disclosure to Authorised Persons in accordance with clause 3, or to a court or other public body in accordance with clause 4; Not use the Confidential Infor mation for any purpose, whether commercial or non commercial, other than the Permitted Purpose; [Make only such limited number of copies of the Confidential Information as are required for the Permitted Purpose, and provide those copies only to Authorised Persons];] and Take proper and all reasonable measures to ensure the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. For the purposes of this Agreement, the following words shall have the following meanings: ‘Information’ shall include information wheth er of a technical, commercial or any other nature whatsoever provided directly or indirectly by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party in oral or documentary form or by way of models, biological or chemical materials or other tangible form or by dem onstrations and whether **only** before, on or after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'only' in the Changed Text significantly restricts the definition of 'Information' to only include information provided before, on, or after the date of the agreement. This directly contradicts the intention of the Contradicted Text, which implies an ongoing exchange of Confidential Information where the Receiving Party must keep the Confidential Information secret and confidential, without temporal limitation. The altered definition creates a clear inconsistency, as the confidentiality obligations in the contradicted section could be interpreted to not apply to information disclosed outside the specified timeframe.

perturbation.original_text: Following the date of any termination of this Agreement, or any return of Confidential information to the Disclosing Party (‘Final Date’), (a) the Receiving Party shall make no further use of the Confidential In formation, and (b) the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement shall otherwise continue in force without limit of time.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the date of any termination of this Agreement, or any return of Confidential information to the Disclosing Party (‘Final Date’), (a) the Receiving Party shall make no further use of the Confidential In formation, and (b) the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement shall otherwise continue in force **for a period of 30 days**.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the duration of the confidentiality obligations post-termination. Changing 'without limit of time' to 'for a period of 30 days' directly contradicts the original indefinite commitment, making the enforceability of confidentiality uncertain after that period and creating in text contradiction with: 6.2 As an exception to its obligations under clause 6.1, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information, in paper form, in the Receiving Party’s legal files for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As an exception to its obligations under clause 6.1, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information, in paper form, in the Receiving Party’s legal files for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement. Following the date of any termination of this Agreement, or any return of Confidential information to the Disclosing Party (‘Final Date’), (a) the Receiving Party shall make no further use of the Confidential In formation, and (b) the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement shall otherwise continue in force **for a period of 30 days**.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the confidentiality obligations post-termination from 'without limit of time' to 'for a period of 30 days'. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which implies an indefinite obligation to comply with the agreement, including retaining a copy of the confidential information for compliance purposes, even after termination. Limiting the obligation to 30 days directly conflicts with the need to retain information to ensure compliance indefinitely, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: To require the Disclosing Party to disclose, co ntinue disclosing or update any Confidential Information;To require the Disclosing Party to negotiate or continue negotiating with the Receiving Party with respect to any further agreement and either party may withdraw from such negotiations at any time without liability; nor
perturbation.changed_text: To require the Disclosing Party to disclose, co ntinue disclosing or update any Confidential Information;To require the Disclosing Party to negotiate or continue negotiating with the Receiving Party with respect to any further agreement and **neither** party may withdraw from such negotiations at any time without liability; nor
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'either' to 'neither', the clause now states that neither party can withdraw from negotiations, contradicting the original agreement that allowed either party to withdraw without liability. It creates in-text contradiction to the entire section as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement does not include, expressly or by implication, any representations, warranties or othe r obligations: To grant the Receiving Party any licence or rights other than as may be expressly stated In this Agreement; To require the Disclosing Party to disclose, co ntinue disclosing or update any Confidential Information;To require the Disclosing Party to negotiate or continue negotiating with the Receiving Party with respect to any further agreement and **neither** party may withdraw from such negotiations at any time without liability; nor As to the accuracy, efficacy, complet eness, capabilities, safety or any other qualities Whatsoever of any information or materials provided under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows either party to withdraw from negotiations without liability. The changed text states that 'neither' party may withdraw from negotiations without liability. This directly contradicts the original agreement and creates a clear contradiction within the provided contradicted text, as the contradicted text contains the changed text. This adheres to the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for taking reasonable action to ensure that its Authorised Persons comply with the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement and shall be liable to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agree ment by such Authorised Persons.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for taking reasonable action to ensure that its Authorised Persons comply with the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement and shall **not** be liable to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agree ment by such Authorised Persons.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'not' creates a direct in-text contradiction. The clause now states the receiving party *is* responsible for ensuring compliance, but simultaneously *is not* liable for breaches by those same persons. This creates ambiguity and makes it difficult to enforce compliance. Which creates direct contradiction to the statement: have been provided with a copy of this Agreement and have agreed with the Receiving Party in writing to comply with the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, and that agreement provides that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to enforce the agreement as a third -party beneficiary;
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its officers, employees (together, ‘Authorised Persons’) who : reasonably need to receive the Confidential Information to enable the Receiving Party to achieve the Permitted Purpose; have been informed by the Receiving Party (a) of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and (b) that the Disclosing Party provided the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party subject to the provisions of a written confidentiality agreement; in the case of the Receiving Party’s officers and employees, have written confidentiality obligations to the Receiving P arty that (a) are no less onerous than the provisions of this Agreement and (b) apply to the Confidential Information, and who have been instructed to treat the Confidential Information as confidential; have been provided with a copy of this Agreement and have agreed with the Receiving Party in writing to comply with the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, and that agreement provides that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to enforce the agreement as a third -party beneficiary; a nd in the case of the Receiving Party’s solicitors, have confirmed that they will treat the Confidential Information as if it were the Receiving Party’s confidential information and therefore subject to the rules of the Law Society concerning client infor mation. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for taking reasonable action to ensure that its Authorised Persons comply with the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement and shall **not** be liable to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agree ment by such Authorised Persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "not" to the original clause, creating a direct contradiction. The original text states the Receiving Party *is* liable for breaches by Authorised Persons, while the changed text states they *are not*. This directly contradicts the original intention and introduces ambiguity about liability, thus making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable, fulfilling the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because it makes enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear. This change directly impacts the contradicted text, which outlines the responsibilities and liabilities related to Authorised Persons, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
